ASP-Q2.desc: The ASP-Q2 is a full-system preamp and 7-band parametric equalizer designed as a precision tuning tool for serious car audio systems. It delivers up to ±12 dB of correction at any point in the frequency spectrum through audiophile-grade internal components and precision pots, producing a maximum output of 7 V RMS. Separate bass-level control balances low frequencies against the highs, and each channel of both Main and Aux inputs has its own independent level adjustment.
narr: Key Features
7-band parametric EQ with ±12 dB boost/cut per band across seven frequency ranges
Subwoofer output with continuously variable crossover from 30 – 300 Hz
Subsonic filter adjustable from 15 – 50 Hz protects woofers from harmful low-frequency energy
2-channel inputs (Main + Aux) with per-channel level trims; 3-channel outputs (Front, Rear, Sub)
7 V RMS maximum output voltage — well above typical head-unit preamp levels
90 dB S/N ratio and 0.03% THD through audiophile-grade signal path components
sections: 40 Hz – 120 Hz (Bass), 100 Hz – 400 Hz (Low), 350 Hz – 900 Hz (Mid-Low), 650 Hz – 1.5 kHz (Mid), 1.2 kHz – 4.5 kHz (Mid-High), 4 kHz – 12 kHz (High), 8 kHz – 18 kHz (Super-High)

ASP-X4.desc: The ASP-X4 is a 2/3/4-way electronic crossover with a 9.5 V line driver built in, accepting 4 channels of input and delivering up to 8 channels of output — including 6 main channels plus a dedicated 2-channel subwoofer output. Its versatile top-panel controls let users reconfigure it as a 2-way, 3-way, or 4-way crossover to suit virtually any system layout, with a switchable 12/24 dB slope and a dash-mountable remote for sub level control.
narr: Key Features
Configurable as 2-way, 3-way, or 4-way electronic crossover from a single unit
4-channel inputs (Rear + Front) driving 8 channels of output (6 main + 2 sub)
9.5 V RMS line driver output voltage — far above typical head-unit preamp levels
HP 1 filter 500 Hz – 5 kHz; BP 2 filter 50 Hz – 5 kHz; BP 3 filter 50 Hz – 3 kHz; LP 4 filter 45 Hz – 450 Hz
Subsonic filter adjustable 15 Hz – 60 Hz; selectable 12/24 dB slope
Dash remote control for channels 7/8 (sub); includes remote power supply
sections: Dash remote control for Ch. 7/8; remote power supply included

ASP-X2.desc: The ASP-X2 is a 2-way electronic crossover with an integrated 9.5 V line driver, taking 2 channels of input and providing 2 channels of output configurable as low-pass/band-pass or high-pass. It includes an LP filter (50 – 450 Hz), an HP filter (450 Hz – 4.5 kHz), a subsonic filter (15 – 60 Hz), and a ×10 switch on output 2 filters for extended range, plus two sets of inputs for fading and a dash remote control.
narr: Key Features
2-way electronic crossover with 9.5 V RMS line driver output
LP filter continuously variable 50 – 450 Hz; HP filter 450 Hz – 4.5 kHz
Subsonic filter adjustable 15 – 60 Hz to protect woofers
Output 2 BPF range extends to 15 Hz – 4.5 kHz via ×10 switch
Two sets of inputs support front/rear fading
Dash remote control included

ASP-L6.desc: The ASP-L6 is a 6-channel, 9.5 V line driver that adds 24 dB of gain to restore signal headroom and dynamic range lost at low head-unit preamp output voltages. It operates as a 6-in / 6-out line driver or, with inputs tied together, as a 2-in / 6-out distribution amplifier driving up to three amplifiers simultaneously from a single RCA pair. Audiophile Elna Silmic II capacitors and Analog Devices OP 275 op-amps keep THD + Noise below 0.01%.
narr: Key Features
6-channel 9.5 V RMS line driver with 24 dB of signal gain
Operates as 6-ch in / 6-ch out line driver or 2-ch in / 6-ch out distribution amplifier
THD + Noise below 0.01% using Elna Silmic II caps and Analog Devices OP 275 op-amps
Dash bass remote control for outputs 5/6
Remote power supply included
sections: Dash bass remote for outputs 5/6; remote power supply included

ASP-L2BT.desc: The ASP-L2 BT is a 2-channel, 9.5 V line driver with Bluetooth streaming, delivering 24 dB of gain across 4 output channels so a single source can drive two amplifiers without Y-adapters. It accepts a direct aux connection from a smart device or MP3 player — ideal for classic cars, motorcycles, or side-by-sides — and includes a dash remote volume control. Elna Silmic II capacitors and Analog Devices OP 275 op-amps hold THD + Noise below 0.01%.
narr: Key Features
2-channel line driver with Bluetooth streaming and 4 RCA outputs at 9.5 V RMS each
24 dB of signal gain eliminates the need to over-drive amplifier input gain
Direct aux input from smart device or MP3 player — no head unit required
Two sets of preamp outputs allow driving separate main and bass amplifiers without Y-adapters
Dash remote volume control included
THD + Noise below 0.01% using Elna Silmic II caps and OP 275 op-amps
sections: 2-channel RCA; direct aux input from smart device/MP3
4-channel RCA (2 sets at 9.5 V RMS each)

ASP-OE8.desc: The ASP-OE8 is an 8-channel active OEM integration adapter that converts factory speaker-level signals into 8 channels of 9.5 V RCA output, preserving the full bandwidth and dynamics that passive LOC adapters strip away. High-end op-amps and capacitors, a low-noise circuit design, built-in channel summing, and a beefy power supply ensure the factory head unit delivers sound quality on par with an aftermarket source unit.
narr: Key Features
8-channel speaker-level to RCA conversion at 9.5 V output — fully active design
High-end op-amps and capacitors with low-noise circuit design
Built-in channel summing for flexible system integration
Dash remote included
Beefy power supply handles high factory amplifier output levels

ASP-OE2.desc: The ASP-OE2 is a 2-channel active OEM integration adapter that converts factory speaker-level signals to 9.5 V RCA outputs while restoring the bass content that many automakers deliberately roll off at higher volumes to protect factory woofers. This bass recovery circuit makes the factory head unit perform like a full-range aftermarket source unit without any passive frequency loss.
narr: Key Features
2-channel active OEM adapter — speaker level in, 9.5 V RCA out
Bass Recovery circuit re-equalizes low frequencies removed by factory head units at higher volumes
Active design eliminates the bass rolloff and mid/high distortion of passive LOC adapters

ASP-OEB.desc: The ASP-OEB is a compact 2-channel active OEM integration adapter, converting factory speaker-level signals directly to 9.5 V RCA outputs in a true active circuit — no passive components in the signal path. Its small footprint makes it easy to tuck behind the factory head unit or in tight installation spaces.
narr: Key Features
True active 2-channel OEM adapter — no passive signal loss
2-channel speaker-level inputs to 2-channel 9.5 V RCA outputs
Compact form factor at 83 × 59 × 26 mm for tight installation spaces

ASP-L2T.desc: The ASP-L2T is a 2-channel line conditioner and noise filter that uses custom-designed, custom-wound high-efficiency transformers to eliminate ground-loop noise by breaking the direct connection between different reference ground systems, without altering signal level, impedance, or bass content. Signal-to-noise exceeds 130 dB and THD + Noise is below 0.1% across the full 20 Hz – 20 kHz range, with 1:1 in/out voltage and impedance.
narr: Key Features
Custom-wound high-efficiency transformers eliminate ground-loop noise without passive bass loss
Signal-to-noise ratio exceeds 130 dB
THD < 0.1% across 20 Hz – 20 kHz
1:1 in/out voltage and impedance — no volume or frequency loss
Compact 100 × 59 × 26 mm footprint

Z-OTG-BTC.desc: The Z-OTG-BT C is a high-quality audio converter combining a USB OTG input (192 kHz/32-bit) with Qualcomm Bluetooth 5.2 (supporting aptX, aptX HD, and aptX Adaptive at 192 kHz/24-bit) and a TI PCM5102A DAC running at up to 384 kHz/32-bit. It outputs simultaneously to optical and coaxial digital outputs (compatible with 48/96/192 kHz digital inputs) and a stereo RCA pair at 1.5 V RMS, powered from a standard 9 – 16 V DC supply.
narr: Key Features
USB OTG input at 192 kHz/32-bit with device charging function
Qualcomm Bluetooth 5.2 with aptX, aptX HD, and aptX Adaptive at 192 kHz/24-bit
TI PCM5102A DAC capable of 384 kHz/32-bit conversion
JRC 5532 high-performance, low-noise dual op-amp in the signal path
Simultaneous optical and coaxial digital outputs (48/96/192 kHz compatible)
Stereo RCA outputs at 1.5 V RMS; powered from 9 – 16 V DC
sections: Qualcomm v5.2 — aptX, aptX HD, aptX Adaptive at 192 kHz/24-bit
Optical and coaxial (48/96/192 kHz digital input compatible)

intro: In 1978, Zapco introduced the world to audiophile signal processing with the iconic PEQ 18-band equalizer — the first high-voltage output processor for the car. Built with high-grade mil-spec slide pots and premium internal components, it showed the industry what in-car sound processing should sound like. That foundation was followed by a series of audiophile-quality processors: the first parametric car EQ, innovative electronic crossovers, a 30-band mono EQ, an advanced noise gate, and even an outboard D/A converter that surpassed most home converters of the era.

Now that most head units and amplifiers have processing built in, Zapco continues to offer outboard processors for the same reason a top home or pro system separates its tuner, preamp, and power amp: each unit doing one job, and doing it right. Zapco processors use the same Elna Silmic II RFS caps and Analog Devices OP 275 op-amps found in the LE/LX competition amplifier series, paired with custom-wound precision pots built to Zapco's own specification. For those who believe the live musical experience is worth the extra effort — and that the sound is what matters most — Zapco has been the driving force since 1974.
